title: Checkpointing fails when run from container in minikube cluster
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

podman checkpoint fails when run from a container in a minikube cluster using crio as the container runtime. I am trying to checkpoint/restore containers that are running in a k8s cluster. To do this, I deploy a container that contains podman (podman added to the ubi8 base image) to the cluster. Then I run the container that I wish to checkpoint from inside this checkpointing container. When I run the checkpoint command 


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Start a minikube cluster (https://minikube.sigs.k8s.io/docs/start/) with crio container runtime: minikube start --container-runtime=cri-o


2. Deploy checkpointing container with podman to cluster:
kubectl apply -f test.yaml

where test.yaml:
[test.yaml.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/9873506/test.yaml.txt)

4. Start container that I want to checkpoint from inside newly deployed checkpointing container: 

podman run -d registry.k8s.io/echoserver:1.4

5. Checkpoint container:

podman container checkpoint -l -e=test.tar.gz

**Describe the results you received:**
checkpoint fails with:

ERRO[0000] container still running
ERRO[0000] criu failed: type NOTIFY errno 0
log file: /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/66c9b99687810ac7d43b4b4db5754d8b49255739eaeb0771fd35ed4941ad00fa/userdata/dump.log
Error: `/usr/bin/runc checkpoint --image-path /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/66c9b99687810ac7d43b4b4db5754d8b49255739eaeb0771fd35ed4941ad00fa/userdata/checkpoint --work-path /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/66c9b99687810ac7d43b4b4db5754d8b49255739eaeb0771fd35ed4941ad00fa/userdata 66c9b99687810ac7d43b4b4db5754d8b49255739eaeb0771fd35ed4941ad00fa` failed: exit status 1

dump.log from error message here:


[dump.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/9873601/dump.log)



**Describe the results you expected:**
Checkpoint file created, able to restore from checkpoint file

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Checkpointing succeeds if minikube uses docker as the container runtime. 

**Output of `podman version`:**

Go Version:   go1.17.7

comments.body:
Can you test with the latest version v4.3 or try using crun instead of runc?

@adrianreber @rst0git PTAL

> Can you test with the latest version v4.3 or try using crun instead of runc?

Trying with crun is worth a try but runc has better checkpoint/restore support overall. We are trying to make sure crun is supported as well as runc.

Thanks for including the CRIU log file that is very helpful. The following is the actual error:
```
(00.152493) Wait for ack 76 on daemon socket
pie: 1: __fetched msg: 76 0 0
pie: 1: Warn  (criu/pie/parasite.c:671): /proc/self/cgroup was bigger than the page size
pie: 1: __sent ack msg: 76 76 -1
pie: 1: Close the control socket for writing
pie: 1: Daemon waits for command
(00.152607) Fetched ack: 76 76 -1
(00.152610) Error (compel/src/lib/infect-rpc.c:72): Command 76 for daemon failed with -1
(00.152613) Error (criu/parasite-syscall.c:456): Parasite failed to dump /proc/self/cgroup
(00.152615) ----------------------------------------
(00.152616) Error (criu/cr-dump.c:1390): Dump core (pid: 1077) failed with -1
```

Too many cgroups seems to be the problem and CRIU cannot handle it correctly. It seems like CRIU just does not allocate enough memory as the situation usually does not happen. I don't know how you start Podman, but would it maybe help to have Podman use the host cgroups. Host in this case probably means the cgroup provided by CRI-O.

Another idea we are working on for some time (there are open PRs) to ignore the internal cgroups by adding
```
manage-cgroups ignore
```
to `/etc/criu/runc.conf` (this only works with runc).

Is this cgroupv1 or v2. Could you try it with the other version to see if CRIU can handle that better.

And then, since you are trying to checkpoint a container in CRI-O. Did you try to use the CRI-O checkpoint support. There is currently a blog post being review about Kubernetes/CRI-O and checkpoint: https://github.com/kubernetes/website/pull/37412

Maybe that is also a solution for you.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Sorry for the very late response, I am able to successfully checkpoint the container inside the cluster when "--cgroupns=private" to the podman run command, this issue can be closed.

Thanks for the help everyone, big fan of your work
